name: Animation
desc: Player swings their arm (left click or server-side swing). Empty packet with no payload.
notes: When the server receives this, it broadcasts S0BPacketAnimation to nearby players. The empty packet just notifies the server of the swing. Client-side swings (swingItem()) are purely local and don't send this packet.

implementation.overview: C0APacketAnimation notifies the server that the player swung their arm. Server broadcasts S0BPacketAnimation to nearby players. Used by KillAura (swing before/after attacks), Scaffold (swing on block place), NoSwing modules (cancel to hide swing), and AutoBlock (swing as part of block-hit cycle). Also used by BadPacketsComponent (Rise) to track attack state, and PingSpoofComponent to delay swing packets.

modules.name: NoSwing / Animation Control (Rise 6.0 KillAura)
modules.purpose: Control when C0APacketAnimation is sent during combat — either send it for every attack, send silently (packet-only without local animation), or disable entirely. NoSwing cancels C0A to hide arm swing from other players while still dealing damage.
modules.how_it_works: Rise 6.0 KillAura sends C0APacketAnimation as part of the attack sequence via PacketUtil.send(). The module's PacketSendEvent listener detects outgoing C0A packets to track swing rate. BadPacketsComponent tracks that swings occur before attacks. NoSwing variants cancel this packet entirely.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayServer.processAnimation(C0APacketAnimation) — server broadcasts S0BPacketAnimation to tracked players via EntityTrackerEntry. Client-side: EntityPlayerSP.swingItem() does NOT send C0A — it only plays local animation. C0A is sent by other modules or player controllers.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: NoSwing (cancelling C0A) is detectable by anti-cheats that verify attacks are preceded by a swing. Grim flags attacks without C0A as invalid combat sequence. Legit players always send C0A before C02 ATTACK.

modules.name: Scaffold Swing (Rise 6.0 / Sigma 4.11)
modules.purpose: Send C0APacketAnimation after placing a block with Scaffold to appear legitimate.
modules.how_it_works: After each block placement, the Module sends C0APacketAnimation so other players see the swing animation. This is the server-side equivalent of swingItem() — without it, the block would appear to place itself.
modules.vanilla_hook: EventSendPacket — send C0A after block placement.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Standard behavior — legitimate players also swing when placing blocks. Not flagged.

modules.name: FreeCam Cancel (Rise 6.0)
modules.purpose: Cancel C0APacketAnimation while in FreeCam to prevent interaction with the world. When the camera is detached from the body, any swings should be from the real body position, not the camera.
modules.how_it_works: While FreeCam is active, all outgoing C0A packets are cancelled. This prevents the player from swinging at entities from the camera position which would reveal the FreeCam usage.
modules.vanilla_hook: PacketSendEvent — FreeCam cancels C0A to prevent body from swinging at camera position.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Canceling C0A+C03+C0B simultaneously looks suspicious server-side (the player appears frozen). Anti-cheats may flag if C02 ATTACK is sent without preceding C0A.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client event hooks: PacketSendEvent (Rise), EventPacket (Sigma), PacketOutEvent (Memeware), onPacketSent (Jigsaw).

Server receive: NetHandlerPlayServer.processAnimation(C0APacketAnimation) → EntityTrackerEntry broadcast S0BPacketAnimation.

Rise 6.0 component tracking: BadPacketsComponent.setSwung() flags that a swing occurred in the current tick, used by other components to determine if the player 'looks legit'.
implementation.client_variations:
Rise 6.0: KillAura sends C0A via PacketUtil.send(). BadPacketsComponent tracks C0A state. FreeCam cancels C0A. Scaffold sends C0A after placement.
Sigma 4.11: Killaura imports C0A, Criticals imports C0A, Scaffold sends C0A, CakeEater sends C0A, Bhop imports C0A.
RektSky B5: Scaffold sends C0APacketAnimation directly.
Jigsaw 0.26: NoSwing cancels C0A in NetworkManager.sendPacket().
